name: Michelle Levesque

handling.tone: French-Canadian / Quebec / New England French heritage. Apr 24 Portuguese SMS was second misfire in batch — switch to English, no awkward apology.
handling.voice_adjustments: Andre voice: warm English, brief. Small event, summer weekend, likely backyard. Don't oversell full-service.
handling.context: Inbound Apr 16 from FB Lead Form. July 3 (day before July 4) — possibly combined birthday + holiday gathering. 25-35 guests = small intimate. $788 ballpark = drop-off pricing. No engagement after Day-1 push.

enrichment.identity_notes: Michelle Levesque = French-Canadian / Quebec / NE French. Levesque is one of the most common French-Canadian surnames; Michelle anglicized. Almost certainly Anglo or French-Canadian heritage, not Portuguese-Brazilian.
win_angles: Small + summer weekend + likely backyard = drop-off or appetizer-heavy
nepq_openers: Reset EN: 'Hey Michelle — switching back to English here. For your birthday on July 3 with 25-35 guests, quick question: at home or venue, and what's the vibe — drop-off-and-relax, light buffet, or something more hosted?'
Frame: 'July 3 — combined with the holiday weekend, or a separate birthday day-before?'
Anchor: 'For 25-35 guests: drop-off ($X), light buffet with light service ($Y), full hosted ($Z). Quick comparison?'
enrichment.strategic_note: Second Portuguese-template misfire (after Esther Manu). One warm English message, doesn't dwell on mistake. Bigger system note: flag Portuguese-default behavior so it stops auto-firing on Anglo names.
